topic: Review of 021 cooperative heavy phases daemon responsiveness packet

findingDetails.title: Trigger backfill still has an unbounded synchronous pre-chunk SELECT
findingDetails.description: REQ-002 says the trigger backfill can never block the loop, but the implementation still loads every eligible memory row with a synchronous .all() before the first cancellation check or chunk yield. The write transaction is chunked, but the pre-chunk read remains corpus-sized, non-cancellable synchronous work.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Correctness pass only. Core traceability was not fully covered because maxIterations=1 and checklist.md is absent from the target Level 1 packet.

results.summary: Most sampled implementation claims resolve, but REQ-002 remains partially unmet by the pre-chunk full-corpus SELECT.
Target packet has no checklist.md; Level 1 packet, so no checklist marks were available to validate.

noveltyJustification: One new contract-safety issue found by comparing REQ-002 against the actual trigger backfill control flow.
ruledOut: Launcher adoption root cause: launcher paths read the fresh maintenance marker before respawn/reap and adopt busy daemons as specified.
Missing marker refresh on timed tail phases: both empty-files and main scan branches enter orphan-sweep, enrichment-repair, trigger-backfill, and near-dup-repair through timedPhase/onPhase.

packets.claim: Trigger backfill still performs an unbounded synchronous full-corpus SELECT before its first cancellation/yield boundary, so REQ-002's never-block/cancellable claim is not fully satisfied.
packets.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed the subsequent phrase-sync chunk loop, cache-hit yield path, cancellation tests, timedPhase scan wiring, and launcher marker-adoption code. The write transaction is chunked and the launcher behavior is correct, but no pagination or cancellation check exists before sourceRows = ...all().
packets.alternativeExplanation: The current corpus may be small enough that the SELECT is fast in practice, and the spec text names the whole-corpus transaction specifically. That does not fully satisfy the stronger acceptance wording that the trigger backfill can never block the loop when enabled.
packets.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or close if the source-row read is paginated/limited with cancellation and yield boundaries, or if a documented hard cap plus regression test proves the SELECT cannot materially block the event loop.
